'''Paul Highton''' (born 10 November 1976) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for Great Britain (Academy), and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for Waterhead A.R.L.F.C. (in [[Oldham]]), [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]/[[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Blue Sox]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

==Background==
Highton was born in [[Oldham]], [[Greater Manchester]], England, and he is the older brother of the rugby league {{rlp|hk}} who played in the 1990s and 2000s for the [[Warrington Wolves]], and the [[Swinton Lions]]; Christopher "Chris" Highton (born {{birth date and age|1978|1|11|df=y}}),<ref name="Chris Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/chris-highton/summary.html|title=Chris Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> and the rugby league {{rlp|fb|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}} who played in the 1990s and 2000s for the [[Warrington Wolves]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], and the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]; David [[Dave Highton|"Dave" Highton]] (born {{birth date and age|1980|1|31|df=y}}).<ref name="David Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/dave-highton/summary.html|title=David Highton Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="Highton accepts drugs ban">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1375712.stm|title=(David) Highton accepts drugs ban|publisher=BBC|date=31 December 2001|access-date=1 January 2002}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Highton played for Waterhead ARLFC prior to turning professional at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[1994–95 Rugby Football League season|1994–95 season]], he represented the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] (Academy) on the 1996 tour down-under, he made his début for [[Featherstone Rovers]] on 5 April 1998, and he played his last match for Featherstone Rovers during the 1998–season, he joined Salford in 1998 after he had played 4 matches for Featherstone Rovers, Paul was to spend the rest of his Career with the Salford club being awarded a testimonial by both club and governing body the RFL for his services to the game

He signed for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 1998 having also played for [[Featherstone Rovers]], and represented [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in the 2000 [[Rugby League World Cup]].

==Post playing==
Highton retired in 2009 and went on to become the clubs Football / Player Welfare Manager. Since retiring, Highton has aligned himself to numerous charities taking up both educational, and ambassadorial roles namely The Sporting Chance Clinic, State of Mind and RLCares. Highton's involvement with RLCares has recently (2016) seen him take on the Challenge of Cycling 3000 miles from Londons Olympic Stadium to the opening Ceremony of the Rio Olympics in Rio de Janeiro. His Epic challenge saw him travel through France, Spain and Portugal before flying over to Recife in Northern Brazil and then cycling the final 1700-miles down the coast to Rio de Janeiro. Highton also continues his involvement with rugby league with his weekly commentary, and summarising on the [[Super League]] with [[BBC Radio Manchester]].
